score.finalText:
Last five inbox emails for bclymer@alleatogroup.com:

1. Vercel — “Approaching your limits: Upgrade now to avoid service disruption”
   - Received: 2026-05-19 06:40:41 UTC
   - Notes: Possible operational action needed; usage warning. Not client-urgent, but time-sensitive.

2. Alleato Group — “ERP Integrations Daily Summary”
   - Received: 2026-05-19 06:16:11 UTC
   - Notes: Internal/system summary. No immediate action evident from the snippet.

3. Kenny / Alleato-related external sender — “Alleato group 2026 Pay Allowance Adjustment, Compensations Plans_REF - 6li4aon8GM”
   - Received: 2026-05-19 01:54:16 UTC
   - Notes: Attachment present. Sender looks unusual/spoof-like; treat as suspicious until verified.

4. Mark Purvis — “Fw: Exol Wilmer”
   - Received: 2026-05-19 01:29:42 UTC
   - Notes: Project-related forwarded message with attachment. Likely routine follow-up.

5. Connor Clark — “Re: Superior Beverage 8319 - In Rack Sprinkler Call Follow Up”
   - Received: 2026-05-19 01:17:59 UTC
   - Notes: Project follow-up; mentions ceiling height not yet available. No urgent escalation indicated.

Urgent-item assessment:
- I did not find a true emergency, legal, security, or client-critical escalation in the last five messages.
- The most notable risk item is the suspicious pay/compensation email with a mismatched-looking sender/domain. That should be verified before opening attachments or replying.

Capability status:
- Live Outlook inbox read succeeded.
- No stale context or tool failure occurred.
- No Teams draft was created because no urgent escalation threshold was clearly met.

Recommended next steps for Megan:
- Review the Vercel usage warning if that site/app matters operationally.
- Verify the suspicious compensation email before interacting with it.
- Open the project follow-ups only if you want a response drafted or summarized further.

Approvals needed:
- None for reading the inbox.
- Approval would be needed only if you want me to draft a reply, Teams escalation, or any send/post action.
